scenario: An Element Template has 12 properties. The team wants to group them visually in the property panel -- common groups like "Authentication", "Endpoint", "Behaviour".
question: How are Element Template properties visually grouped?

options.text: Relying solely on property order for implicit grouping is a partial approach that lacks the explicit structure the groups feature provides. When properties are simply listed sequentially, modelers must scroll through all properties to find what they need, with no visual section boundaries or collapsible groupings. The groups feature goes beyond order by providing labeled section headers and collapsible sections in the property panel.

Element Template JSON supports a groups top-level array where each group is defined with an id and label. Individual properties reference their group via the group field matching the group's id. Both Web Modeler and Desktop Modeler render groups as collapsible, labeled sections in the property panel. This grouping feature has been a supported part of the Element Template specification for multiple Camunda 8 versions.

Define groups in the Element Template JSON (a top-level array of group definitions with IDs and labels), then assign each property to a group via the group field on the property. Web Modeler / Desktop Modeler renders properties under collapsible group headers in the property panel. Improves UX for templates with many properties -- modelers find what they need faster.

Separate templates would require modelers to apply multiple templates to the same Service Task, which is an unusual pattern and may not even be supported depending on the modeler version. The groups feature was specifically designed to solve this problem within a single template definition without requiring template fragmentation.

a.text: Incorrect. Element Template groups feature for organisation: JSON structure: `json { "$schema": "...", "name": "Slack Connector", "groups": [ { "id": "auth", "label": "Authentication" }, { "id": "target", "label": "Target Channel" }, { "id": "message", "label": "Message Configuration" }, { "id": "advanced", "label": "Advanced Settings" } ], "properties": [ { "label": "OAuth Token", "type": "String", "group": "auth", ... }, { "label": "Channel Name", "type": "String", "group": "target", ... }, { "label": "Message Body", "type": "Text", "group": "message", ... }, ... ] } ` Web Modeler rendering: Property panel shows collapsible sections per group. Group order in JSON determines display order. Modelers expand / collapse groups as needed. UX benefits: Quickly locate properties (less scrolling through long flat lists). Logical clustering signals which properties relate. Collapse rarely-used groups to focus on common ones. Best practices: Meaningful group names: "Authentication" beats "Group 1". Don't over-group: 3-5 groups typically optimal; too many fragments. Order groups by use frequency: most-used first.
